definition: A step-by-step method used to solve a problem or perform a task
example: We need a faster algorithm for sorting this data.

definition: A way for different programs or systems to communicate and share data or functionality
example: We need to connect to the payment API before we can process transactions.

definition: A collection of items stored in a specific order
example: The list of users is stored in an array.

definition: The process of verifying the identity of a user or system
example: Authentication is failing when users try to log in.

definition: The process of determining what a user is allowed to do
example: The user passed authentication but failed authorization.

definition: The part of a system that handles logic, data processing, and storage behind the scenes
example: The issue is on the backend, the frontend is working fine.

definition: The amount of data that can be transmitted over a connection in a given time
example: We’re hitting bandwidth limits during peak hours.

definition: A system of representing data using only 0s and 1s
example: The file is stored in binary format.

definition: An error or flaw that causes a program to behave incorrectly
example: There’s a bug causing the app to crash.

definition: A storage layer used to temporarily store frequently accessed data for faster access
example: We should cache this response to improve performance.

definition: A blueprint used to create objects like users or products in a system
example: We created a class for users so every new account follows the same structure.

definition: A saved change in version control systems
example: I just made a commit with the latest fixes.

definition: A tool that converts source code into a form a computer can run
example: The compiler is throwing an error on this line.

definition: Handling multiple tasks at the same time
example: We need better concurrency to handle more users.

definition: Small data stored on a user's device by a website
example: The login state is saved in a cookie.

definition: A system used to store, organize, and retrieve data efficiently
example: The user data isn’t saving to the database.

definition: The process of finding and fixing errors in code
example: I’ve been debugging this issue all morning.

definition: The process of releasing software to a live environment
example: The deployment failed last night.

definition: An external piece of code that a project relies on
example: We need to update this dependency.

definition: A system that translates domain names into IP addresses
example: DNS is not resolving the domain correctly.

definition: The process of converting data into a secure format
example: We use encryption to protect user data.

definition: A specific address where an API can be accessed
example: This endpoint returns user details.

definition: A setup where code runs, like development or production
example: It works in dev but not in the production environment.

definition: An error that occurs during program execution
example: The app throws an exception when input is invalid.

definition: A structured set of tools and rules for building applications
example: We’re using a framework to speed up development.

definition: The part of a system that users interact with directly on screen
example: I’ll fix the frontend so the button displays correctly.

definition: A named set of steps that performs a specific task when used
example: This function handles user input.

definition: A system for tracking changes in code over time
example: Push your changes to Git.

definition: A way to organize data for faster searching
example: We need an index to speed up queries.

definition: Data provided to a system for processing
example: The input format is incorrect.

definition: A defined way for different parts of a system to interact
example: We need a clear interface between these components.

definition: Repeating a set of steps multiple times
example: This loop handles each iteration of the data.

definition: A format used to structure and exchange data
example: The API returns data in JSON.

definition: The delay before a transfer of data begins
example: We’re seeing high latency on requests.

definition: A collection of pre-written code that can be reused
example: We’re using a library for handling dates.

definition: A way to repeat the same steps multiple times based on a condition or count
example: This loop runs through all the items.

definition: The part of a system that stores data temporarily while running
example: The app is using too much memory.

definition: Code that runs between request and response handling
example: The middleware checks authentication.

definition: A separate piece of code that handles a specific part of a program
example: This logic is in a module for user accounts.

definition: A group of connected systems that can communicate
example: The network connection is unstable.

definition: A single unit or device in a network or system
example: Each server acts as a node in the system.

definition: A structured collection of related data and behavior
example: The user is represented as an object with their details.

definition: Software with source code that anyone can view and modify
example: This project is open source so anyone can contribute.

definition: Data produced by a system after processing
example: The output of the function is shown on screen.

definition: A set of rules for how data is transmitted
example: HTTP is a common protocol for websites.

definition: A request for data from a database
example: This query is taking too long to return results.

definition: The period when a program is running
example: The error only happens at runtime, not during build.

definition: A system that provides data or services to other systems
example: The server is down right now so the app isn’t working.

definition: A collection of elements arranged in a specific order, often last-in-first-out
example: The error shows up in the stack trace from the stack.

definition: A named value that can be stored and changed while a program runs
example: This variable changes when the user updates their profile.
